rxn_class_summary: Photoinduced palladium-catalyzed three-component asymmetric carboamination of 1,3-dienes with C(sp3)-H substrates and amines to give chiral allylic amines

key_intermediates.name: aryl radical / Pd(I) pair from ArBr SET reduction
alkyl radical (from HAT on C(sp3)-H substrate, e.g. benzyl radical)
allyl radical intermediate III from radical addition to 1,3-diene

electron_flow: Blue-light excited Pd(0)Ln* reduces ArBr by single-electron transfer to give an aryl radical and LnPd(I)Br; the aryl radical abstracts a C(sp3)-H hydrogen (HAT) to generate an alkyl radical, which adds to the 1,3-diene to give an allyl radical that recombines with Pd(I) to form a pi-allyl Pd(II) complex, finally undergoing nucleophilic allylic substitution by the amine to release product and regenerate Pd(0).
rate_determining_step: Intermolecular hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) from the C(sp3)-H substrate to the aryl radical (KIE = 1.9)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; TEMPO-aryl adduct 101 and TEMPO-benzyl adduct 102 detected by HRMS, supporting aryl and benzyl radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: trans-1-phenyl-2-vinylcyclopropane (103, radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 104 formed in 31% yield, 64% ee, supporting radical addition to the alkene

probe_reagents.name: D8-toluene (D8-1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel KIE kH/kD = 1.9, implicating HAT in the turnover-limiting step

probe_reagents.name: Ir(ppy)3 (photosensitizer swap)
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed when used in place of Pd catalyst, indicating Pd is essential beyond simple photosensitization

probe_reagents.name: dark / no light control
probe_reagents.observation: No product even at 100 °C, confirming photoexcitation requirement

probe_reagents.name: omission of ArBr or Pd/L
probe_reagents.observation: No product, confirming both Pd catalyst and aryl bromide oxidant are required

notes: Bulky 2,6-di-tert-butyl-substituted aryl bromides are used to suppress direct aryl-radical addition to the diene (side product 105), favoring the HAT pathway. Pi-allyl Pd(II) intermediate IV detected by HRMS. Chirality is set by chiral phosphine ligand (e.g., BINAP/xyl-BINAP) during nucleophilic attack on the pi-allyl Pd(II) complex.
